pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page for the economics and fraud research portal with global search, dashboard-style summaries, and shortcuts into all major sections.
primary_functions: Run a global search across publications by typing a query into the main search bar (e.g., cryptocurrency fraud, LIBOR manipulation, Ponzi scheme).
Provide quick navigation links to Publications, Datasets, Authors, and Events sections.
Surface shortcuts to key user assets (e.g., links to reading lists, dataset cart, citation exports, collections, planner).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit global search form or click 'View all results' under publication previews
Click 'Datasets' quick link card
Click 'Authors' quick link card
Click 'Events' quick link card
Click a recent reading list in the dashboard summary
Click dataset cart summary
Click article collections summary
Click citation export lists summary
Click bookmarks counter
Click alerts/notifications counter
Click upcoming event in planner teaser

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Home' link in the main header navigation
Directly via index.html or site root as the main landing page

pages.name: Publications Search & Results
pages.description: Search and browse publications including working papers, journal articles, and other research outputs, with comprehensive filtering and saved search alerts.
primary_functions: Search publications by keyword (e.g., cryptocurrency fraud, tax evasion Latin America, Ponzi scheme, financial statement fraud detection, LIBOR manipulation).

Filter results by publication type (e.g., working paper, journal article), year range, citations count, access type (e.g., open access), language, journal impact factor, and publication status (e.g., retracted).
Sort results by relevance, publication date (newest or oldest first), and citation count.
Open individual publication detail pages from the results list.
Save the current filtered search as a named alert/saved search (e.g., Ponzi Schemes 2010+) and configure notification settings.
Indicate availability of full-text downloads (e.g., PDF icon) to help users pick articles for collections.

incoming_params.description: Initial keyword query for the publications search (e.g., 'cryptocurrency fraud', 'tax evasion Latin America').
Optional ID of a saved search alert whose parameters should initialize the search.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a publication title in the results list
Click 'Manage alerts' or similar link near the Save Search control
Click site logo or 'Home' breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Publications' link in the main header navigation
Submitting the publications search form reloads this page with updated query/filter parameters
Deep links using query parameters (e.g., from saved search alerts) open this page pre-filtered

pages.name: Publication Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single publication or working paper with full metadata, citation tools, and saving options such as reading lists, collections, bookmarks, and citation exports.
primary_functions: Display full bibliographic information, abstract, citation counts, impact metrics, access status, and download options for the publication.
Add the publication to a reading list, including creating a new list (e.g., Crypto Fraud 2024) or adding to an existing one.
Add the publication to an article collection, including creating a new collection (e.g., FS Fraud Detection 20182023) or adding to an existing one.
Bookmark the publication and apply user-defined tags (e.g., case study) for later reference.
Generate formatted citations in multiple styles (e.g., APA) and copy or download them.
Add a formatted citation (e.g., APA) to a citation export list, including creating a new export list (e.g., Tax Evasion Assignment) or selecting an existing one.
Navigate back to the originating results list via breadcrumbs or back link.

incoming_params.description: ID of the publication to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to search results' breadcrumb
Click 'View this reading list' after adding the publication to a list
Click 'View this collection' after adding the publication to a collection
Click 'View citation export list' after adding an APA citation
Click 'View all bookmarks' after bookmarking the publication
Click an author name in the author list

access_methods.description: Accessed via publication_detail.html?id={publicationId} links from search results, lists, and bookmarks
May be linked directly from external sources using the publication ID

pages.name: Datasets Search & Results
pages.description: Search and filter research datasets, including fraud-related micro-level data, with visibility into key metrics like observations and variables.
primary_functions: Search datasets by keyword (e.g., credit card fraud).
Filter datasets by data type (e.g., micro-level transaction data), license type (e.g., non-commercial research), coverage, and other dataset attributes.
Sort dataset results by number of observations (high to low), relevance, or recency.
Open dataset detail pages in the same or separate browser tabs from the results list.
Indicate key summary metrics (e.g., number of observations) directly in the results to aid comparison.

incoming_params.description: Initial dataset search query (e.g., 'credit card fraud').
Optional saved search alert ID for dataset-scoped alerts.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a dataset title in the results list (including ctrl+click to open in new tab)
Click 'View cart' after adding a dataset to the cart from the results list
Click site logo or 'Home' breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Datasets' link in the main header navigation
Submitting the dataset search form reloads this page with updated query/filter parameters
Deep links using query parameters (e.g., from help examples or alerts) open this page pre-filtered

pages.name: Dataset Detail
pages.description: Detail page for an individual dataset with summary statistics, license information, and cart controls for download or access requests.
primary_functions: Display dataset metadata including description, number of observations, number of variables, geographic and temporal coverage, and license terms.
Allow users to compare the number of observations and variables across datasets to decide which is larger and sufficiently compact (e.g., under 50 variables).
Add the dataset to the dataset cart for later download or request.
Provide links or instructions for accessing or requesting the dataset according to its license.

incoming_params.description: ID of the dataset to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View dataset cart' after adding the dataset to the cart
Click 'Back to dataset results' breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessed via dataset_detail.html?id={datasetId} links from dataset search results or the dataset cart
May be linked directly from external documentation using the dataset ID

pages.name: Dataset Cart
pages.description: Central place to review and manage datasets selected for use, including download and request workflows.
primary_functions: List all datasets added to the dataset cart with key metadata (e.g., title, number of observations, license).
Remove datasets from the cart or clear the cart entirely.
Initiate download or request access workflows for selected datasets, subject to license terms.
Provide a summary of total datasets and any outstanding actions needed (e.g., agreements to terms).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a dataset title in the cart to review its details
Click 'Add more datasets' from the cart

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Dataset Cart' link in the main header navigation
Accessible via the 'Dataset Cart' link in the footer
Linked from home/dashboard summaries and dataset detail pages

pages.name: Authors Search & Results
pages.description: Search and browse researchers and authors in economics and financial crime topics, with emphasis on money laundering experts and publication metrics.
primary_functions: Search authors by name or topic keyword (e.g., money laundering).
Filter authors by topical tags or expertise areas (e.g., Money Laundering), institution, or region.
Sort authors by number of publications (high to low), name, or citation metrics.
Open individual author profile pages from the results list.
Indicate high-level metrics such as publications count to help identify experts with at least five publications.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an author name in the results list
Click site logo or 'Home' breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Authors' link in the main header navigation
Accessible via the 'Authors' link in the footer
Submitting the author search form reloads this page with updated query/filter parameters
Deep links with author search parameters can preconfigure this page

pages.name: Author Profile
pages.description: Profile page for an individual researcher with biography, metrics, list of publications, and follow/unfollow controls.
primary_functions: Display author biography, affiliations, and key metrics such as total number of publications and citation counts.
List the author's publications with links to individual publication detail pages.
Allow the user to follow or unfollow the author to track their new work.
Provide navigation back to the author search results via breadcrumbs or back link.

incoming_params.description: ID of the author whose profile is displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View all in Publications search' to see the author's work in the publications search UI
Click 'Back to author results' breadcrumb
Click on an individual publication in the author's publications list

access_methods.description: Accessed via author_profile.html?id={authorId} links from author search results or publication detail pages
May be linked directly from external profiles using the author ID

pages.name: Events & Conferences Search & Results
pages.description: Search and filter academic events and conferences related to economics and financial crime, including financial crime conferences in Europe.
primary_functions: Search events and conferences by keyword (e.g., financial crime).
Filter events by region/location (e.g., Europe), date range, registration fee (e.g., maximum $500), and event type.
Sort events by start date (earliest first), relevance, or registration deadline.
Open individual event detail pages from the results list.

incoming_params.description: Start of the event date range filter (ISO date string, e.g., '2025-09-01').
End of the event date range filter (ISO date string, e.g., '2025-12-31').
Maximum registration fee filter (numeric, assumed in USD or equivalent).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an event title in the results list
Click site logo or 'Home' breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Events' link in the main header navigation
Submitting the event search form reloads this page with updated query/filter parameters
Deep links with event search parameters can preconfigure this page

pages.name: Event Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single conference or event with scheduling, registration, and planner integration.
primary_functions: Display full event information including title, location, dates, agenda overview, and registration fee.
Add the event to the user's planner (e.g., Save to My Events) for future reference.
Provide links or guidance to register for the event on external sites if applicable.
Navigate back to the events search results via breadcrumbs or back link.

incoming_params.description: ID of the event whose details are displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View in planner' after adding the event to the planner
Click 'Back to events' breadcrumb
Click site logo or 'Home' breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessed via event_detail.html?id={eventId} from event search results or planner entries
May be linked directly from external event promotion using the event ID

pages.name: Reading Lists
pages.description: Manage named reading lists of publications and working papers, such as topic-specific sets like 'Crypto Fraud 2024'.
primary_functions: Display all reading lists the user has created (e.g., Crypto Fraud 2024) with counts of items in each.
Open a specific reading list to view contained publications and navigate to their detail pages.
Create, rename, or delete reading lists.
Reorder or remove items within a reading list.

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a reading list to open by default.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a publication title within a reading list

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Reading Lists' link in the main header navigation
Accessible via the 'Reading Lists' link in the footer
Linked from the home/dashboard summary and publication detail actions
Deep-linked reading lists can be opened with readingLists.html?readingListId={id}

pages.name: Citation Export Lists
pages.description: Manage lists of formatted citations for export to reference managers or assignments, such as 'Tax Evasion Assignment'.
primary_functions: Display all citation export lists (e.g., Tax Evasion Assignment) with the number of citations in each.
Open an export list to review included citations and edit or remove individual entries.
Export citations in various formats (e.g., RIS, BibTeX, plain text) for use in reference managers or documents.
Rename or delete citation export lists.

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a citation export list to open by default.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a publication title associated with a citation in a citation export list

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Citation Exports' link in the footer
Linked from home/dashboard and from publication detail citation actions
Deep-linked export lists can be opened with citation_exports.html?citationExportListId={id}

pages.name: Article Collections
pages.description: Manage curated collections of articles, such as thematic reading sets on fraud detection (e.g., 'FS Fraud Detection 2018–2023').
primary_functions: Display all article collections created by the user (e.g., FS Fraud Detection 20182023) with counts of included items.
Open a collection to view its articles and navigate to each article's detail page.
Create, rename, or delete article collections.
Add or remove publications from collections and adjust their order within a collection.

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of an article collection to open by default.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a publication title in an article collection

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Collections' link in the footer
Linked from home/dashboard and publication detail pages when managing collections
Deep-linked collections can be opened with collections.html?articleCollectionId={id}

pages.name: Bookmarks
pages.description: Central view of all bookmarked publications with tag-based organization, including items tagged for case-study use.
primary_functions: List all bookmarked items (e.g., retracted LIBOR manipulation article) along with applied tags such as case study.
Filter or search bookmarks by tag, keyword, or publication type.
Edit bookmarks by adding, removing, or renaming tags.
Remove items from bookmarks and navigate to bookmarked items' detail pages.

incoming_params.description: Optional tag filter to apply to bookmarks (e.g., 'case study').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a bookmarked publication's title

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Bookmarks' link in the footer
Linked from the home/dashboard bookmarks summary and publication detail confirmation messages
Bookmarks page can be deep-linked with filters like tag=case+study

pages.name: Alerts & Saved Searches
pages.description: Manage saved searches and in-site notification alerts for new research updates across publications, datasets, authors, and events.
primary_functions: List all saved searches/alerts (e.g., Ponzi Schemes 2010+) with a summary of their filters and last run date.
Edit alert settings including name, notification type (e.g., in-site/dashboard notifications), and frequency (e.g., weekly).
Enable, disable, or delete individual alerts.
Manually run a saved search to view updated results in the Publications Search & Results page.

incoming_params.description: Optional alert ID to highlight and/or auto-expand when opening the page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View results in Publications' for a publications-scoped alert
Click 'View results in Datasets' for a datasets-scoped alert
Click 'View results in Events' for an events-scoped alert
Click 'View results in Authors' for an authors-scoped alert

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Alerts & Notifications' link in the footer
Linked from the home/dashboard alerts summary and the Publications Save Search workflow
Specific alerts can be focused via alerts.html?savedSearchAlertId={id}

pages.name: Event Planner
pages.description: Personal planner for conferences and events the user has saved, supporting status tracking (planned, registered, attended, cancelled).
primary_functions: Display a list or calendar view of events added to the planner, including titles, locations, and dates.
Navigate from planner entries to corresponding event detail pages.
Remove events from the planner if plans change.
Provide summary information about upcoming events (e.g., next event date, total planned events).

incoming_params.description: Optional status filter for planner entries (e.g., 'planned', 'registered').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an event title in the planner list or calendar

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Planner' link in the main header navigation
Accessible via the 'Planner' link in the footer
Linked from event detail pages after adding an event to the planner
Planner view may be filtered by status via planner.html?status=planned

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the economics and fraud research website, including scope, data sources, and project credits.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose, scope, and intended audience of the research portal.
Describe data sources, curation standards, and coverage (e.g., working papers, datasets, conferences).
Provide organizational or project credits and acknowledgments.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' link in the footer
May be linked directly from external sites describing the project

pages.name: Help & FAQ
pages.description: User assistance and FAQs for using search, filters, and saving tools, including step-by-step flows aligned with common research tasks.
primary_functions: Provide step-by-step guidance on common tasks such as creating reading lists, using the dataset cart, setting alerts, and managing collections.
Offer FAQ entries about features like citation exports, following authors, and planner usage.
Give troubleshooting tips for search and filtering issues.
Provide information on how to contact the site maintainers or support channel if needed.

incoming_params.description: Optional help topic ID to open directly (e.g., from contextual 'Help' links on other pages).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a help link that demonstrates running a publications search
Click a help link that demonstrates using the dataset cart
Click a help link that demonstrates managing alerts
Click a help link that demonstrates managing article collections

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Help & FAQ' link in the footer
Can be opened directly as a general help landing page
Specific help topics can be deep-linked via help.html?topicId={id}

header_links.description: Homepage with global search and quick access to all sections
Search and browse publications, working papers, and articles
Find and follow researchers and authors
Search and browse conferences and events
View and manage saved reading lists of publications
Review datasets selected for use or download
View conferences and events saved to your planner

footer_links.description: Information about the research portal and its purpose
User help, guides, and frequently asked questions
View all bookmarked items and tags
Manage citation export lists for assignments or reference managers
Manage saved searches and in-site alerts
Review and adjust datasets selected for download or request
Review conferences and events saved to your planner
Search for researchers and manage followed authors
